summary: Russia launched a massive combined airstrike on 15 June, deploying 50 rockets and over 580 Shahed‑type drones, primarily targeting Kyiv and also striking Dnipro and Kharkiv. The attack caused four civilian deaths in Kyiv, four rescuers and one civil defence specialist killed in Kharkiv, and extensive fires at cultural sites. Ukraine’s air defence intercepted 632 targets, including 50 rockets and 582 drones.
key_points: Russia executed a coordinated strike with 50 rockets and 582 Shahed‑type drones, focusing on Kyiv and also hitting Dnipro and Kharkiv (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
Four civilians were killed in Kyiv’s Obolon, Holosiiv and Solomiansky districts during the attack (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
Four DSNS rescuers and one civil‑defence specialist were killed in Kharkiv while responding to the strike (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
Ukraine’s air defence shot down 632 hostile objects, including 5 anti‑ship missiles, 15 Iskander‑M ballistic missiles and 30 Kh‑101 cruise missiles (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
Shahed drones were reported over Kyiv districts Darniсky, Poznyaki, Rusanyvka, Pechersk and Klov (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67806, @mon1tor_ua/67805, @mon1tor_ua/67804).
The Kyiv Pechersk Lavra was hit by a Russian “Geranium‑2” drone, a Shahed variant (Source: @UkraineNow/81941).
Russia began launching cruise missiles from the first Tu‑160 bomber (Source: @eRadarrua/96777).
Ukrainian authorities began recovery of two ballistic missile fragments from the Dovzhenko film studio site (Source: @UkraineNow/81942).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a massive combined airstrike on 15 June, employing 50 rockets and over 580 Shahed‑type drones, with the main focus on Kyiv and secondary strikes on Dnipro and Kharkiv. The assault killed four civilians in Kyiv and five emergency responders in Kharkiv, ignited numerous fires and damaged cultural landmarks. Ukrainian air defence claimed 632 interceptions, including rockets, missiles and drones.

points: Ukrainian air defence reported shooting down 632 hostile targets, comprising 50 rockets, 5 anti‑ship missiles, 15 Iskander‑M ballistic missiles, 30 Kh‑101 cruise missiles and 582 Shahed‑type drones (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
Four civilians died in Kyiv’s Obolon, Holosiiv and Solomiansky districts as a result of the strike (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
Four DSNS rescuers and one civil‑defence specialist were killed while assisting victims in Kharkiv after the attack (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
Significant cultural sites were hit, including the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, the Dovzhenko film studio, and the National Art Arsenal, causing extensive damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
Ukrainian forces recovered fragments of two ballistic missiles at the Dovzhenko studio site (Source: @UkraineNow/81942).
Russia employed a mixed salvo of 50 rockets, 34 Iskander‑M/С‑400 ballistic missiles, 30 Kh‑101 cruise missiles and over 600 Shahed‑type drones in the 15 June attack (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
Launches originated from multiple Russian regions, including Bryansk, Kursk, Orel, Crimea’s TOT‑AR and Vologda (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
A Russian Tu‑160 strategic bomber was reported to have launched cruise missiles for the first time (Source: @eRadarrua/96777).
Russian Shahed drones were observed heading toward Kyiv districts Darniсky, Poznyaki, Rusanyvka, Pechersk and Klov (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67806, @mon1tor_ua/67805, @mon1tor_ua/67804).
A Russian “Geranium‑2” drone, a Shahed variant, struck the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra (Source: @UkraineNow/81941).
Shahed drones were detected over the Dnipro area, with a group reported moving from Pavlograd toward Dnipro (Source: @kpszsu/65249).
Ukrainian air defence intercepted multiple Shahed drones and rockets aimed at Kharkiv, including attacks on the Kharkiv residential districts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67773).
Four DSNS rescuers and one civil‑defence specialist were killed in Kharkiv while responding to a Russian strike (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
Russian missile launches from Bryansk, Kursk and Orel regions targeted eastern Ukrainian cities, including Kharkiv (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
Ukrainian forces reported extensive fire fighting efforts in Kharkiv following repeated strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
No specific northern front activity was detailed in the provided messages.
No specific southern front activity was detailed in the provided messages.
Ukrainian air defence claimed 632 interceptions, including 5 3M22 “Zircon” anti‑ship missiles, 15 Iskander‑M ballistic missiles and 30 Kh‑101 cruise missiles (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
Russian Shahed‑type drones were the most numerous aerial threat, with 582 detected and engaged (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
A Russian “Geranium‑2” drone, a Shahed variant, struck the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra (Source: @UkraineNow/81941).
Ukrainian radar channels reported multiple Shahed incursions over Kyiv districts Darniсky, Poznyaki, Rusanyvka, Pechersk and Klov (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67806, @mon1tor_ua/67805, @mon1tor_ua/67804).

Ukrainian forces continue to receive Patriot air‑defence missiles, though a video suggested a Patriot missile may have fallen on a residential building (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30898, @ukr_leaks_eng/30913).

key_developments: Russia launched a coordinated strike on 15 June using 50 rockets, 34 Iskander‑M ballistic missiles, 30 Kh‑101 cruise missiles and over 600 Shahed drones, focusing on Kyiv and also hitting Dnipro and Kharkiv (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
Ukrainian air defence reported shooting down 632 hostile targets, including 5 Zircon anti‑ship missiles and 15 Iskander‑M missiles (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
Four civilians were killed in Kyiv’s Obolon, Holosiiv and Solomiansky districts during the attack (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
Four DSNS rescuers and one civil‑defence specialist died in Kharkiv while responding to the strike (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
Shahed drones were observed over multiple Kyiv districts, including Darniсky, Poznyaki, Rusanyvka, Pechersk and Klov (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67806, @mon1tor_ua/67805, @mon1tor_ua/67804).
A Russian “Geranium‑2” drone struck the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, a key cultural site (Source: @UkraineNow/81941).
Russia began launching cruise missiles from a Tu‑160 bomber, marking the first such use (Source: @eRadarrua/96777).
sections.threat_assessment: The massive combined strike demonstrates Russia’s capability to coordinate large‑scale missile and drone attacks from multiple launch zones, overwhelming Ukrainian air defences and targeting both civilian and cultural infrastructure. Ukrainian interception success shows robust air‑defence performance, yet the high volume of Shahed drones and missile types indicates a persistent threat that can cause casualties and infrastructure damage despite defenses. Continued use of strategic assets such as the Tu‑160 suggests an escalation in long‑range strike capacity, raising operational risk for Ukrainian command centres and critical sites across the frontlines.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine’s emergency services mobilised over 1,200 rescuers in Kyiv and deployed additional rapid‑response units, while air‑defence units engaged hundreds of aerial threats, reflecting a coordinated national response. Internationally, the reported recovery of missile fragments and damage to cultural sites may prompt further diplomatic pressure on Russia and increased military aid, particularly in air‑defence systems. The ongoing civilian assistance program for displaced Kyiv residents underscores the domestic humanitarian response to the attack’s impact.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of launch sites in Bryansk, Kursk, Orel and Crimea remains critical for early warning of future combined attacks. The prevalence of Shahed‑type drones across multiple fronts indicates a sustained supply line that should be targeted for disruption. The use of the Tu‑160 for cruise missile launches marks a new operational pattern that requires tracking of strategic bomber movements. Continued verification of missile fragment recoveries can aid in identifying specific weapon systems and their origins.
